title: Ensure that the --audit-log-path argument is set

input_row.title: Ensure that the --audit-log-path argument is set
input_row.description: Enable auditing on the Kubernetes API Server and set the desired audit log path.
input_row.rationale: Auditing the Kubernetes API Server provides a security-relevant chronological set of records documenting the sequence of activities that have affected system by individual users, administrators or other components of the system. Even though currently, Kubernetes provides only basic audit capabilities, it should be enabled. You can enable it by setting an appropriate audit log path.
input_row.audit: Run the following command on the Control Plane node: ps -ef | grep kube-apiserver Verify that the --audit-log-path argument is set as appropriate.
input_row.remediation: Edit the API server pod specification file /etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube- apiserver.yaml on the Control Plane node and set the --audit-log-path parameter to a suitable path and file where you would like audit logs to be written, for example: --audit-log-path=/var/log/apiserver/audit.log
input_row.default_value: By default, auditing is not enabled.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The control specifies the need to verify the `--audit-log-path` argument for the Kubernetes API server, which is a standard configuration property that can be queried via Kubernetes APIs or kubectl.
- The audit section mentions using a command to check the API server process, which indicates that this property can be programmatically verified.
- The rationale emphasizes the importance of enabling auditing, which is a standard security practice in Kubernetes.
- The remediation provides a clear method for setting the `--audit-log-path`, indicating that this is a configurable property of the API server.

**PROGRAM NAME:** k8s_api_server_audit_log_path_set

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Use the Kubernetes API to check the command-line arguments of the API server.
- The specific API call would involve retrieving the configuration of the API server pod, typically found in the `kube-system` namespace.
- The command to use would be:
  ```bash
  kubectl get pod -n kube-system -l component=kube-apiserver -o jsonpath='{.items[*].spec.containers[*].command}'
  ```
- This command retrieves the command-line arguments used to start the kube-apiserver container.
- The expected configuration property to validate is the presence of `--audit-log-path` with a valid file path.
- Pass criteria: The output should include `--audit-log-path=/var/log/apiserver/audit.log` or a similar valid path.
- Fail criteria: The output does not include `--audit-log-path` or it is set to an invalid path.
